Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails in the INBOX folder (via search_emails) with query "NorthWind" to find Pam Kowalski's April 24 final-warning email (email_email_7168baed8438), which names the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and the Emilia Cruz piano damage, references a missed April 14 recovery-plan commitment, states NorthWind has had preliminary conversations with PathFinder, and demands a retention proposal from David Chen by EOD Friday.
OE 2: Search emails in the SENT folder (via search_emails) with query "NorthWind" to find Elena Rostova's April 15 reply (email_email_ccaaecb4e47a) instructing David to keep Catalina primary on the account and to escalate only if there is a confirmed renewal threat or client departure risk -- confirming Elena's last direct engagement with the account was April 15.
OE 3: Search emails in the DRAFT folder (via search_emails) with query "NorthWind" to find Catalina Dubois's unsent draft (email_email_ab99acca3399) addressed to David Chen, admitting she is underwater on the account with six active relocations and asking for temporary coverage on client comms or move-level coordination.
OE 4: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages) with query "NorthWind" to find David Chen's April 26 post in the #executive channel (channel_id: C004) reporting that Pam Kowalski bypassed Catalina entirely, named both service failures, called out the missed April 14 recovery promise, confirmed NorthWind is in active save mode, and stated he needs to deliver a concrete retention proposal by EOD Friday covering corrective actions, claim resolution, future coverage, and commercial consideration.
OE 5: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages) with query "NorthWind" in #operations (C006) and #customer-support (C007) to surface internal discussion around the Raj Patel IT escort failure, the Emilia Cruz piano damage support log, and Catalina's capacity triage request -- establishing the full internal picture of what has deteriorated since April 15.
OE 6: Search CRM for the NorthWind company record (via crm_search_companies with name "NorthWind") to retrieve company_northwind, then search for associated deals using both company_id "company_northwind" (via crm_search_deals) and dealname "NorthWind" (via crm_search_deals) to surface all four NorthWind deals: deal_northwind_denver_expansion (dealstage: qualifiedToBuy, company_id: company_northwind), deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 (dealstage: contractSent, company_id: company_northwind), deal_northwind (dealstage: proposalMade, company_id: null), and deal_northwind_won (dealstage: closedWon, company_id: null) -- the company_id search alone misses the two orphaned Phase 1 deals; the dealname search is required to find them.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks invoices (via quickbooks_search_invoices) for NorthWind Technologies (CustomerRef: cust_northwind) to find inv_northwind_01 (INV-2026-003, $60,000 Phase 1 enterprise contract, Balance: $60,000, Due 2026-02-19) and inv_northwind_denver_02 (INV-2026-0412, $14,700 Denver expansion, Balance: $14,700, Due 2026-05-01) -- no QuickBooks write actions are required by this prompt, but reviewing outstanding balances confirms the full financial picture of the account for the Elena briefing.
OE 8: Retrieve CRM engagements for NorthWind (via crm_list_engagements with company_ids: ["company_northwind"]) to find eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context, which contains the full account recovery context: the original $60,000 contract for five relocations at $12,000 each, Q1 CSAT of 5/10 overall with service failures in Phase 1 apartments, the two Denver expansion relocation details for Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz, and the acknowledged risks around chain-of-custody protocol and piano subcontractor coordination that set the stage for the April failures.
OE 9: List Airtable records (via airtable_list_records) in base appMoveOpsOps001, table name "Relocations" (tblRelocations01), and search for NorthWind Technologies to find all eight NorthWind relocation records: five Phase 1 records (Ryan Caldwell recReloc00000014, Lisa Petrosky recReloc00000015, Derek Nash recReloc00000016, Amira Okonkwo recReloc00000020, Ben Schaefer recReloc00000021 -- all Status: Completed), one audit note record (recRajPatelAuditNote0411 -- Status: In Progress, April 10 single-day entry documenting the escort breach incident), and two Denver expansion records still showing Status "In Progress": recRajPatelChicagoDenver (Raj Patel, Chicago to Denver, move window April 21-23) and recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver (Emilia Cruz, Chicago to Denver, move window April 14-18) -- the two Denver expansion records need to be updated to "Completed."
OE 10: Search Airtable records (via airtable_search_records) in base appMoveOpsOps001, table name "Client Accounts" (tblClientAccts01), field "Client" with value "NorthWind Technologies" to find record recAcct000000064, which shows Total Relocations as 5 -- this count is stale and must be updated to 7 to reflect the two Denver expansion relocations for Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz.
OE 11: Search Linear issues (via linear_list_issues) with query "NorthWind" or "Patel" to find two tickets with incorrect states: issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (OPS-3, "DOT Docs -- Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS," state_id: state_done) and linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 (OPS-482, "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations," state_id: null) -- OPS-3 is incorrectly marked Done while the PHMSA inquiry is active, and OPS-482 was created April 24 but has never been activated.
OE 12: Look up Elena Rostova's contact (via contacts_search_contacts with query "Elena Rostova") to confirm email address elena.rostova@moveops.com, and look up David Chen's contact (via contacts_search_contacts with query "David Chen") to confirm email address david.chen@moveops.com, before sending the briefing email and creating the calendar event.
OE 13: Check Elena Rostova's calendar (via calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to) for the April 27 to May 1 window to identify an open slot available before end of day Friday for the call with David Chen -- the base calendar data shows no conflicting events during this period.
OE 14: Update the stage of CRM deal deal_northwind_denver_expansion (via crm_update_deal_stage) with id "deal_northwind_denver_expansion" and dealstage "closedwon" to correct the stale qualifiedToBuy status -- the two Denver expansion relocations for Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz have been executed.
OE 15: Update the stage of CRM deal deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 (via crm_update_deal_stage) with id "deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2" and dealstage "closedwon" to correct the stale contractSent status -- both deals for the same two-relocation Denver expansion must be aligned to closedwon.
OE 16: Update the stage of CRM deal deal_northwind (via crm_update_deal_stage) with id "deal_northwind" and dealstage "closedwon" to correct the stale proposalMade status -- this orphaned Phase 1 deal record ($60,000, five enterprise relocations all completed) was never closed and has company_id: null, making it invisible to company_id-based searches; it was found only via the dealname search.
OE 17: Update Airtable record recRajPatelChicagoDenver (via airtable_update_records) in base appMoveOpsOps001, table_id "tblRelocations01," setting the Status field to "Completed" -- the Raj Patel Chicago to Denver move concluded April 23 and the record has remained incorrectly set to In Progress.
OE 18: Update Airtable record recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver (via airtable_update_records) in base appMoveOpsOps001, table_id "tblRelocations01," setting the Status field to "Completed" -- the Emilia Cruz Chicago to Denver move concluded April 18 and the record has remained incorrectly set to In Progress.
OE 19: Update Airtable record recAcct000000064 (via airtable_update_records) in base appMoveOpsOps001, table_id "tblClientAccts01," setting the Total Relocations field from 5 to 7 -- the five original Phase 1 relocations plus the two Denver expansion relocations for Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz bring the correct total to seven.
OE 20: Update Linear issue issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (via linear_update_issue) with id "issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind" and state set to "In Progress" -- the DOT documentation ticket for the Raj Patel hazmat shipment is incorrectly marked Done while the active PHMSA inquiry (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) is ongoing with a June 5 documentation deadline.
OE 21: Update Linear issue linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 (via linear_update_issue) with id "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1" and state set to "In Progress" -- the NorthWind retention response plan ticket (OPS-482) was created April 24 following Pam Kowalski's escalation but has never been given an active state, leaving a priority-1 ticket effectively invisible in the workflow.
OE 22: Send a briefing email (via send_email) with sender "priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com" and recipient "elena.rostova@moveops.com" covering what has changed since Elena's April 15 engagement: Pam Kowalski's April 24 formal final-warning email to David Chen (bypassing Catalina entirely) naming the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and Emilia Cruz piano damage, NorthWind's disclosure that they have had preliminary conversations with PathFinder, David's EOD Friday deadline to deliver a full retention proposal, and the data corrections completed across CRM deals, Airtable relocation records and client account, and Linear tickets.
OE 23: Create a calendar event (via calendar_add_calendar_event) with attendees ["elena.rostova@moveops.com", "david.chen@moveops.com"], a title referencing the NorthWind account call, and a start and end datetime scheduled in an open slot between April 27 and April 30, 2026 -- placing the call before the EOD Friday May 1 deadline that David cited for the NorthWind retention proposal.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

25 The model must pass a dealname parameter related to 'NorthWind' (or similar) when calling crm_search_deals, in addition to or as an alternative to a company_id filter, to surface orphaned CRM deals that have no company_id value.
query construction
 ✓✓✗✗✗✓ 50%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a crm_search_deals call with parameter {"dealname": "NorthWind"}, which is a dealname-based query distinct from any company_id filter. This surfaced all four deals including deal_northwind (company_id: null).
✓Run #2The agent called crm_search_deals with parameter {"dealname": "NorthWind"}, a name-based query that successfully surfaced all 4 NorthWind deals including deal_northwind (which has company_id: null and would be invisible in a company_id-filtered search).
✗Run #3The agent only called crm_search_deals with company_id 'company_northwind', which returned only 2 deals. No dealname-based search was performed for 'NorthWind', so deal_northwind (with company_id: null) was never discovered, and its stale proposalMade stage was never corrected.
✗Run #4The agent only called crm_search_deals with company_id 'company_northwind'. No dealname-based search was ever performed, meaning deal_northwind (which has company_id: null) was never discovered.
✗Run #5The agent only called crm_search_deals with company_id='company_northwind'. There was no dealname-based search for 'NorthWind'. As a result, deal_northwind (company_id: null) was never surfaced or updated.
✓Run #6The agent called crm_search_deals with parameter dealname 'NorthWind', which successfully surfaced deal_northwind (the orphaned deal with company_id: null) that would not appear in a company_id-filtered search.
